html:
Exclusive to:  

The Snakeskin Jacket is a craftable Dress Item introduced in the Shipwrecked. It is the DLC's version of the Rain Coat. It requires 2 Snakeskins, 2 Vines, and 2 Bone Shards to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. The Sewing Kit repairs 62.5% of its durability.

When worn during Rain, it protects against Wetness gain by 90%. Additionally, it insulates the player against Lightning strikes and the electric shocks of Jellyfish. It is also a tier 1 warm clothing, increasing the time a character can be without a fire during the Winter before Freezing. It has an insulation factor of 60, the same as the Rabbit Earmuffs.

Hamlet

The Snakeskin Jacket is also included in the Hamlet DLC, however, it has a very different appearance as Snakeskin is obtained from Vipers. It is especially useful by offering protection against Lightning strikes produced by Thunderbirds. 

 Trivia

  • Based on character examination quotes, the Snakeskin Jacket seems to have the same consistency of a leather jacket, explaining its mild insulation and high wetness protection.
  • The jacket appears to be sleeveless, as the characters appear to have their arms in the back, and the item itself has no sleeves.

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Willow wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wendy wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • WX-78 wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Woodie wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wes wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Maxwell wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Webber wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Walani wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Warly wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wilbur wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wilba wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wormwood wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wheeler wearing a Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wilba wearing the Hamlet version of the Snakeskin Jacket.
  • Wheeler wearing the Hamlet version of the Snakeskin Jacket.

It is also a tier 1 warm clothing, increasing the time a character can be without a fire during the Winter before Freezing. It has an insulation factor of 60, the same as the Rabbit Earmuffs.

The Snakeskin Jacket is also included in the Hamlet DLC, however, it has a very different appearance as Snakeskin is obtained from Vipers. It is especially useful by offering protection against Lightning strikes produced by Thunderbirds.

It is the DLC's version of the [[Rain Coat]]. It requires 2 [[Snakeskin]]s, 2 [[Vine]]s, and 2 [[Bone Shards]] to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. The [[Sewing Kit]] repairs 62.5% of its durability. When worn during [[Rain]], it protects against [[Wetness]] gain by 90%. Additionally, it insulates the player against [[Lightning]] strikes and the electric shocks of [[Jellyfish]]. It is also a ''tier 1'' warm clothing, increasing the time a character can be without a fire during the [[Winter]] before [[Freezing]]. It has an [[Freezing#Mechanics|insulation factor]] of 60, the same as the [[Rabbit Earmuffs]]. =={{Pic|32|Hamlet icon}} Hamlet== The Snakeskin Jacket is also included in the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, however, it has a very different appearance as Snakeskin is obtained from [[Viper]]s.
